findings.title: Cosine similarity definition missing ‖z‖ in denominator
findings.snippet: DEFINITION 1: The cosine similarity of two nonzero vectors y and z is ρ(y, z) = (y · z) / ‖y‖.
findings.explanation: The formal definition omits ‖z‖ from the denominator. Standard cosine similarity is (y · z) / (‖y‖ ‖z‖). The error is confirmed by the paper's own proof of Proposition 2 (Appendix), which correctly expands ρ(y*, u^ℓ(G)) = (y* · u^ℓ(G)) / (‖y*‖ ‖u^ℓ(G)‖) — using both norms.
findings.fix: Change the definition to ρ(y, z) = (y · z) / (‖y‖ ‖z‖).

findings.title: Self-referential change-of-variables formula (missing tilde on b̃_i)
findings.snippet: Performing the change of variables b_i = [τ − b_i] / 2 and β = −β̃/2 (with the status quo equal to b̂_i = [τ − b̃_i] / 2)
findings.explanation: The right-hand side of the change-of-variables formula uses b_i, making the equation self-referential and meaningless. The status-quo formula on the same line correctly uses the local-public-goods variable b̃_i; by analogy, the change-of-variables formula should also map b̃_i to b_i.
findings.fix: Replace b_i = [τ − b_i] / 2 with b_i = [τ − b̃_i] / 2.

findings.title: Missing summation index i in cost-function formula
findings.snippet: K(B) = φ( Σ_{∈ N} σ^B_{ii} )  if E[b] = b̄
findings.explanation: The summation subscript reads '∈ N' with no index variable, so the notation is malformed. Every other summation over N in the paper uses 'i ∈ N'.
findings.fix: Change Σ_{∈ N} to Σ_{i ∈ N}.

findings.title: ĥ𝐛 labelled 'status quo actions' instead of 'standalone marginal returns'
findings.snippet: As long as the status quo actions b̂ are positive, this constraint will be respected for all C less than some Ĉ
findings.explanation: Throughout the paper b̂ is consistently and formally defined as the status quo vector of standalone marginal returns (not actions). The equilibrium actions depend on b̂ through â* = [I − βG]⁻¹b̂. Calling b̂ 'actions' contradicts established terminology and could mislead readers.
findings.fix: Replace 'status quo actions b̂' with 'status quo standalone marginal returns b̂'.

findings.title: Inconsistent terminology for b̂: 'standalone incentives' / 'status quo incentives' vs. 'standalone marginal returns'
findings.snippet: … how it depends on the network, the nature of spillovers, the status quo incentives, and the budget. [line 17] … the status quo vector of standalone incentives … [line 244]
findings.explanation: The paper formally introduces 'standalone marginal return' as the precise term for b_i and uses it consistently in most places, but several passages substitute 'standalone incentives' or 'status quo incentives' with no indication that these are synonyms. This creates terminology drift that could confuse readers about whether a distinct quantity is intended.
findings.fix: Replace all occurrences of 'standalone incentives' and 'status quo incentives' (when referring to b̂) with the paper's canonical term 'standalone marginal returns'.

findings.title: Missing 's' in plural possessive 'individual'' (abstract)
findings.snippet: How does a planner optimally target interventions that change individual's private returns to investment?
findings.explanation: 'Individual's' is a singular possessive, but the sentence refers to returns across multiple individuals. The correct plural possessive is 'individuals''.
findings.fix: Change 'individual's private returns' to 'individuals' private returns'.

findings.title: Truncated plural possessive 'individual'' in body text
findings.snippet: the vector of individual' eigenvector centralities in the network of strategic interactions
findings.explanation: The word 'individual'' is missing the letter 's' before the apostrophe. It should be 'individuals''.

findings.title: Missing hyphen in 'firstorder'
findings.snippet: The firstorder condition for individual i's action to be a best response is
findings.explanation: 'First-order' is a compound modifier and requires a hyphen when used attributively before a noun.

findings.title: Misspelling 'faciliates' → 'facilitates'
findings.snippet: The last part of the assumption is technical; it holds for generic status quo vectors b̂ … and faciliates a description of the optimal intervention
findings.explanation: 'Faciliates' is missing the letter 't'; the correct spelling is 'facilitates'.

findings.title: Mixed singular/plural figure reference 'Figures … and Figure'
findings.explanation: The sentence switches between the plural 'Figures' and the singular 'Figure' for items in the same cross-reference list, which is grammatically inconsistent.

findings.title: Singular possessive 'individual's' where plural possessive is needed
findings.snippet: Shocks to individual's standalone marginal returns create variability in the players' equilibrium actions.
findings.explanation: The sentence refers to shocks affecting multiple individuals, so the plural possessive 'individuals'' is required.
findings.fix: Change 'individual's standalone marginal returns' to 'individuals' standalone marginal returns'.

findings.title: Missing comma between co-authors in reference list
findings.snippet: Galeotti, A., B. Golub S. Goyal (2020): "Supplement to 'Targeting Interventions in Networks'"
findings.explanation: A comma is missing after 'B. Golub', separating the second and third authors. All other multi-author entries in the reference list use commas consistently.
findings.fix: Change 'B. Golub S. Goyal' to 'B. Golub, S. Goyal'.

findings.title: Ambiguous phrasing 'negatively correlated among neighboring nodes'
findings.snippet: the bottom eigenvectors (with larger values of ℓ) tend to be negatively correlated among neighboring nodes
findings.explanation: 'Negatively correlated among neighboring nodes' is ambiguous: it is unclear whether the eigenvector entries of a single node are correlated with each other, or whether the entries of adjacent nodes are anticorrelated with one another. The intended meaning (entries of adjacent nodes have opposite sign) is better expressed with 'across'.
findings.fix: Replace with 'the bottom eigenvectors tend to have entries that are negatively correlated across neighboring nodes' or 'the bottom eigenvectors tend to assign opposite-sign entries to neighboring nodes'.
